calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-28-2015 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jul-10-2015 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Sep-01-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-02-2015 Set For Trial On Jul-13-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default, Vacate Judgment, And For Leave To File Answer
Off Calendar Jan-16-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. EDIT ALEXANDRYAN of PERSOLVE, LLC, Tel No. (818) 534-3108 appearing telephonically for the Plaintiff;
Atty. AARON PALLEY, Tel No. (415) 890-3162 appearing for Defendant Sonia Coronado-Brown.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SONIA CORONADO-BROWN'S Motion to Set Aside Default, Vacate Judgment, and for Leave to File Answer is granted. The default and default judgment are set aside. Defendant shall file her proposed answer within 10 days of entry of this order. Defendant submits evidence that she did not respond to the complaint because of "mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect." (CCP sec. 473(b).) Substitute service was effectuated on Defendant's mother, who speaks little English, and mistakenly did not understand the papers and did not give them to her daughter. (Declaration of Norma Coronado; Declaration of Sonia Coronado-Brown, pars., 2, 4.) There is conflicting evidence presented about whether Defendant was informed by Plaintiff about the lawsuit. (Declaration of Dawn Stafseth, pars. 2-4; Declaration of Sonia Coronado-Brown, pars. 2-3.) "Because the law strongly favors trial and disposition on the merits, any doubts in applying section 473 must be resolved in favor of the party seeking relief from default." (Elston v. City of Turlock (1985) 38 Cal.3d 227, 233.) Further, where the party in default moves promptly to seek relief and no prejudice to the opposing party will result from setting aside the default and letting the case go to trial on the merits, "very slight evidence will be required to justify a court in setting aside the default." (Elston v. City of Turlock (1985) 38 Cal.3d 227, 233.) Plaintiff does not establish that it will be prejudiced by setting aside the default and default judgment and permitting the case to proceed on the merits. Plaintiff's request for attorney's fees and cost in this matter is denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/AJR)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-02-2014 Off Calendar
